arts.ttl: Arsenal handed huge Premier League title boost as major Liverpool problem revealed
arts.sum: Liverpool have been top of the table on six occasions on Christmas Day in the Premier League era, but have only gone on to lift the trophy on one of those occasions. Arsenal are six points off the pace at the top of the Premier League table, and they've played a game more than current leaders Liverpool too.

arts.ttl: Kieran Tierney stands alone in Arsenal squad amid Mikel Arteta trophy ribbing
arts.sum: The ex-Celtic man has yet to kick a ball this season but was part of the last Gunners squad to lift a major trophy

arts.ttl: Second club in mix for Arsenal man – Situation to get busy in coming days as suitors pile up
arts.sum: Clube Atlético Mineiro are also interested in signing forgotten Arsenal man Marquinhos on loan in the January transfer window. That’s according to Trivela, who say the Brazilian side have entered the competition for the Arsenal man after Internacional made the first move. The 21-year-old right winger is currently on loan at Fluminense but is set The post Second club in mix for Arsenal man – Situation to get busy in coming days as suitors pile up appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Marcus Rashford can secure dream Arsenal shirt number if he completes Man Utd transfer
arts.sum: Rashford has gone on the record admitting that he is ready to quit Old Trafford in search of a "new challenge". In the interview, Rashford said: "For me, personally, I think I'm ready for a new challenge and the next steps."

arts.ttl: Paul Scholes' ominous Man Utd prediction amid Marcus Rashford to Arsenal transfer rumours
arts.sum: Paul Scholes believes that Marcus Rashford is a victim of his own success as the Manchester United academy graduate now looks set to leave the club. "I think the thing with Marcus is, he was so good two years ago when he had that season where he scored 30 goals, he's just never got back to that level again.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta slammed for 'stalling' one Arsenal star as huge transfer demand made
arts.sum: Paul Scholes has claimed that Gabriel Martinelli has "gone backwards" over the last couple of years and believes that Arsenal need to add more goals from midfield if they are going to win the Premier League title this season. "I look at Martinelli and actually think probably three years ago when this team was just getting built, it felt like Odegaard, [Bukayo] Saka and Martinelli were all at the same sort of level, looked like they could go on and be top players.

arts.ttl: Three unanswered Arsenal questions Mikel Arteta must address against Crystal Palace - Tom Canton
arts.sum: Despite the obvious progress and impressive level that Arsenal have reached these past two-plus years in the league, Arteta has failed to see his side be equally as strong in the cups. While reaching the quarter-finals of the Champions League last season was a first in 14 years, the defeat to Bayern Munich still stung and felt like a missed opportunity.

arts.ttl: Virgil Van Dijk, William Saliba among most played in 2024
arts.sum: The CIES Football Observatory has published data on the most used players over the course of the calendar year, with Barcelona's Jules Koundé atop the list and Liverpool's Virgil van Dijk and Real Madrid midfielder Federico Valverde among the top five. Player usage and rest has become a topic of considerable concern in recent weeks and months, particularly after Manchester City's Ballon d'Or-winning midfielder Rodri said players could go on strike if the football calendar continues to become more congested.

arts.ttl: Martin Keown sends message to Mikel Arteta with joke about Arsenal transfer target
arts.sum: Arsenal legend Martin Keown has commented on the Gunners’ need for a new striker, but has suggested one high-profile target would not be the answer. Speaking to the Oxford Mail, former Arsenal defender Keown discussed the links with Sporting Lisbon striker Viktor Gyokeres, but raised one concern. arts.ttl: Raheem Sterling: Mikel Arteta has 'no trust' in Arsenal "panic buy" as Chelsea transfer decision made
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has no trust in "panic buy" Raheem Sterling after refusing to bring him on against Everton, Charles Watts tells Sports Mole
